title: galium mollugo var. guilhelmii
intro: Galium mollugo var. guilhelmii, also known as creeping bedstraw, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, creeping bedstraw is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or added to topical creams for localized treatment.
